#5516 

Tries to make assignment with Array Item as Array reference.

MRE 
```
program array_indices_array_item_assignment 
integer :: A(3) = [1,2,3]
integer :: X(2) = [1,2]
integer :: Y = 2
A(X) = Y
print * , A
end program
```

LFortran result
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort3/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
2    2    3
```

Still Array Section (like A(X(:)) = Y ) is not addressed in this PR , which I will address in subsequent #5516 

Enables Assignment with ArraySection as Array Reference.

MRE 1 :- 
```
program test 
integer :: A(3) = [1,2,3]
integer :: X(2) = [1,2]
integer :: Y = 2
A(X(:)) = Y
print * , A
end program
```

LFortran output :- 
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort3/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
2    2    3
```

MRE 2 :-
```
program test 
integer :: A(4) = [1,2,3,4]
integer :: X(3) = [1,2,3]
integer :: Y = 0
A(X(1:2)) = Y
print * , A
end program
```

LFortran output :- 
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort3/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
0    0    3    4

        A([1,2]) = [5,5]
    end subroutine
end program
```

Example 2 : 
```fortran
program test
    integer :: A(4) = [1,2,3,4]
    call temp(A)
    print *, A
contains
    subroutine temp(A)
        integer, intent(inout) :: A(:)
        A([1,2]) = A([2,1])
    end subroutine
end program
```

LFortran output : 
```console
(lf) jinu2110@JNSLAP:/mnt/c/lfortran$ lfortran a.f90 
1    2    3    4

end program

MRE 
```
program test
integer :: A(2) = [1,2]
integer :: n = 2
integer :: dim = 1
A = sum(spread(A, dim, n), dim=dim)
print *, A
end program
```

MRE 
```
program test
integer :: A(2) = [1,2]
integer :: n = 2
integer :: dim = 1
A = sum(spread(A, dim, n), dim=dim)
print *, A
end program
```

It falis for case where dim (sum argument) is provided with compile - time value . That generates an error due to some other issue.
ASR generates for below MREs.

MRE - 1 :
```
program test
integer :: A(2) = [1,2]
integer :: n = 2
integer :: dim = 1
A = sum(spread(A, dim, n), dim=1)
print *, A
end program
```

Output
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort3/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

MRE - 2 : 
```
program test
integer :: A(2) = [1,2]
integer :: n = 2
integer :: dim = 1
A = sum(spread(A, dim, n), 1)
print *, A
end program
```

Output
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort3/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2544804302/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```fortran
program test1
    integer :: B(4) = [1,2,3,4]
    B([2,1,3,4]) = B
    print *, B
end program
```

Test 2: 
```fortran
program test2
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    A(:, :) = A(:, [1,2])
    print *, A
end program
```

Test 3: (BinOp) 
```fortran
program test3
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    integer :: A2(2,2) = reshape([4,5,6,7],[2,2])
    A([2,1],[2,1]) = A - A2
    print *, A
end program
```

Test 4: (Reshape)
```fortran
program test4
    integer :: A(2,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4],[2,2])
    integer :: B(4) = [1,2,3,4]
    A([2,1],[2,1]) = reshape(B, [2,2])
    print *, A
end program
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2563843611","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849#issuecomment-2563843611","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5849","id":2563843611,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Y0SYb","user":{"login":"AnshMehta1","id":142878451,"node_id":"U_kgDOCIQm8w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/142878451?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1","html_url":"https://github.com/AnshMehta1","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-27T16:17:48Z","updated_at":"2024-12-27T16:17:48Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> Test 1:

> 
> I don't think so, `tmp` is usually set at very end and if `SemanticAbort` is thrown `tmp` would still be nullptr. But it'll be good to set `tmp` and `tmp_vec` as nullptr if `--continue-compilation` is on.

Made tmp as null and tmp_vec as empty . This changes are made within catch block so as to not to hinder the behaviour of code flow when their is no Semantic Fortran Code : 
```fortran
module my_module
contains
    subroutine cb(a,terminate)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: a
        logical, intent(out), optional :: terminate
    end subroutine cb
end module my_module

program test
    use my_module
    logical :: terminate_var
    call temp(cb)

contains

    subroutine temp(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        logical :: terminate_var
        call call_back(terminate = terminate_var)   
    end subroutine temp

end program test
```

LFortran Error : 
```
semantic error: Keyword arguments are not implemented for generic subroutines yet, symbol type: 9
  --> b.f90:21:9
   |
21 |         call call_back(terminate = terminate_var)
   |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 
```

Works with GFortran.

#5513 

Earlier , we were not visiting arguments of Elemental Function . This PR tries to incorporate this thing in array_op.

MRE 1
```
program test 
  integer :: A(3) = [1,2,3]
  logical :: X(3)
  integer :: tol = 2
  X = abs(A) <= max(tol, tol * maxval(abs(A)))
  print *, X
end program
```

Output 
```
(lf) ansh_mehta@DESKTOP-VJGRLON:/mnt/d/lfortran2/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
T    T    T
```

MRE 2
```
program test 
  integer :: A(3) = [1,2,3]
  logical :: X(3)
  integer :: tol = 2
  X = abs(A) <= max(tol,maxval(abs(A)))
  print *, X
end program
```

Output 
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort2/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
T    T    T
```

MRE 3
```
program test 
integer :: A(3,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4,5,6], [3,2])
integer :: X(2)
X = abs(sum(A, dim=1))
print *, X
end program
```

Output 
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort2/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
6    15

#5513 

Earlier , we were not visiting arguments of Elemental Function . This PR tries to incorporate this thing in array_op.

MRE 1
```
program test 
  integer :: A(3) = [1,2,3]
  logical :: X(3)
  integer :: tol = 2
  X = abs(A) <= max(tol, tol * maxval(abs(A)))
  print *, X
end program
```

Output 
```
(lf) ansh_mehta@DESKTOP-VJGRLON:/mnt/d/lfortran2/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
T    T    T
```

MRE 2
```
program test 
  integer :: A(3) = [1,2,3]
  logical :: X(3)
  integer :: tol = 2
  X = abs(A) <= max(tol,maxval(abs(A)))
  print *, X
end program
```

Output 
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort2/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
T    T    T
```

MRE 3
```
program test 
integer :: A(3,2) = reshape([1,2,3,4,5,6], [3,2])
integer :: X(2)
X = abs(sum(A, dim=1))
print *, X
end program
```

Output 
```
(lf) lordansh@LordAnsh:~/dev/lfort2/lfortran$ ./src/bin/lfortran try.f90
6    15

implicit none


real, allocatable :: array(:)
integer :: ndim

ndim = 10

allocate(array(-(ndim/2):ndim))

print *, lbound(array > 0, 1)
call f(array > 0)

contains

    subroutine f(array1)
        logical, intent(in) :: array1(:)
        print *, lbound(array1, 1), ubound(array1, 1)
        if( lbound(array1, 1) /= 1 ) error stop
        if( ubound(array1, 1) /= 16 ) error stop
    end subroutine

end program arrays_39

Minimal Reproducible Example

```fortran
PROGRAM main
   IMPLICIT NONE
   INTEGER :: i
   INTEGER :: j

   i = 1
   j = 0

   PRINT *, i/j

END PROGRAM main
```
### Output
#### LFortran
```console
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ lfortran ./examples/example.f90
-1
```
It is strange that GFortran does not catch this error either and fails with a `Floating point exception (core dumped)`.
#### GFortran
```console
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ ./a.out

Program received signal SIGFPE: Floating-point exception - erroneous arithmetic operation.

Backtrace for this error:
#0  0x798077623c5d in ???
#1  0x798077622d85 in ???
#2  0x79807724531f in ???
        at ./signal/../sysdeps/unix/sysv/linux/x86_64/libc_sigaction.c:0
#3  0x5d640d5921d1 in ???
#4  0x5d640d59223c in ???
#5  0x79807722a1c9 in __libc_start_call_main
        at ../sysdeps/nptl/libc_start_call_main.h:58
#6  0x79807722a28a in __libc_start_main_impl
        at ../csu/libc-start.c:360
#7  0x5d640d5920b4 in ???
#8  0xffffffffffffffff in ???
Floating point exception (core dumped)
```

error is different for different types.

### Integer array
```fortran
program main
   print *, reshape([1, 2, 3, 4], [2, 2])
end program
```
```console
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ gfortran ./examples/example.f90 && ./a.out
           1           2           3           4
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ lfortran ./examples/example.f90
-595967272
```

### Real array
```fortran
program main
   print *, reshape([1.33, 2.45, 3.78, 4.62], [2, 2])
end program
```
```console
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ gfortran ./examples/example.f90 && ./a.out
   1.33000004       2.45000005       3.77999997       4.61999989    
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ lfortran ./examples/example.f90
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

%array = type { i32*, i32, %dimension_descriptor*, i1, i32 }
%dimension_descriptor = type { i32, i32, i32 }
.
.
.
code generation error: asr_to_llvm: module failed verification. Error:
FPExt only operates on FP
  %60 = fpext [4 x float]* %fixed_size_reshaped_array to double



Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

### Logical array
```fortran
program main
   print *, reshape([.false., .true., .true., .false.], [2, 2])
end program
```
```console
(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ gfortran ./examples/example.f90 && ./a.out

(base) saurabh-kumar@Awadh:~/Projects/System/lfortran$ lfortran ./examples/example.f90

MRE:
```fortran
program test
implicit none

real(4) :: B(3, 3)
integer(4) :: A(3)
A = [3,1,2]
B = transpose(B(:, A))

end program
```

GFortran compiles this, but LFortran gives the error:
```
semantic error: `transpose` accepts arrays of rank 2 only, provided an array with rank, 1
 --> test.f90:7:15
  |
7 | B = transpose(B(:, A))
  |               ^^^^^^^


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

#4397 

Fortran Code : 
```fortran
module my_module
contains
    subroutine cb(a,terminate)
        implicit none
        integer, intent(in), optional :: a
        logical, intent(out), optional :: terminate
    end subroutine cb
end module my_module

program test
    use my_module
    logical :: terminate_var
    call temp(cb)

contains

    subroutine temp(call_back)
        implicit none
        procedure(cb) :: call_back
        logical :: terminate_var
        call call_back(terminate = terminate_var)   
    end subroutine temp

end program test
```

LFortran Error : 
```
semantic error: Keyword arguments are not implemented for generic subroutines yet, symbol type: 9
  --> b.f90:21:9
   |
21 |         call call_back(terminate = terminate_var)
   |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 
```

Works with GFortran.

newcomers"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-10-26T07:05:34Z","updated_at":"2024-10-26T11:53:28Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The f2023 standard C805 says this  program is not valid because ```linelen``` is not a specification expression. But lfortran 0.41.0 compiles it and prints '?' 
```
  integer            :: linelen = 80
  character(linelen) :: line
  line = '?'
  print *, trim(line)
end program
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5173/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5173/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2439549719","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5173#issuecomment-2439549719","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5173","id":2439549719,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6RaJMX","user":{"login":"AnshMehta1","id":142878451,"node_id":"U_kgDOCIQm8w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/142878451?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1","html_url":"https://github.com/AnshMehta1","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AnshMehta1/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-26T11:53:26Z","updated_at":"2024-10-26T11:53:26Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> @AnshMehta1 would you like to take a look into this?
> 
> We need to take care of this between AST -> ASR. The AST for the simplified MRE:
> 
> ```fortran
> program main
>     integer :: linelen = 2
>     character, dimension(2) :: line
> end program
> ```
> 
> is
> 
> ```clojure
> (TranslationUnit
>     [(Program
>         main
>         ()
>         []
>         []
>         [(Declaration
>             (AttrType
>                 TypeInteger
>                 []
>                 ()
>                 ()
>                 None
>             )
>             []
>             [(linelen
>             []
>             []
>             ()
>             2
>             Equal
>             ())]
>             ()
>         )
>         (Declaration
>             (AttrType
>                 TypeCharacter
>                 []
>                 ()
>                 ()
>                 None
>             )
>             [(AttrDimension
>                 [(1
>                 linelen
>                 DimensionExpr)]
>             )]
>             [(line
>             []
>             []
>             ()
>             ()
>             None
>             ())]
>             ()
>         )]
>         []
>         []
>     )]
> )
> ```
> 
> Note the `AttrDimension` attribute inside the `Declaration` node for `line`. We are concerned with it. Here, the start dimension is `1` and end dimension is `linelen`, which is a non-constant variable. We essentially want to allow only constant values here. So the only correct dimension values can be either a `parameter` annotated integer variable, or a constant integer. We process the dimensions while declaration through the function `process_dims()` inside `ast_common_visitor.h`. We need to throw an error when the above stated condition is false, for both the start and end dimension.
> 
> This should take care of the MRE in [#5173 (comment)](https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5173#issuecomment-2439503478) also.

Surely, I will work on it in the evening.
